Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Emily Fowler, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, T:415.398.0200, for defendant/moving party;
James Mills, Esq. of the Law Offices of James Mills, T:510.521.8748, for plaintiff/opposing party, by Court Call;
Denise Lopez Campa, Esq. of Dentons US LLP, T:213.623.9300, for defendant Otis Elevator.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, and for reasons stated on the record, the Court ruled on DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, as follows:

DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied.

DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC.'s Alternate Motion For Summary Adjudication is Granted as to the 2nd Cause Of Action - premises liability; and Denied as to the 1st cause of action - negligence.

Parties are to work on a mutually acceptable order and submit it to the Court for review and approval.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jun-28-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James Mills, of Law Office of James Mills, Tel. 510.521.8748, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Emily Fowler, of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, Tel. 415.398.0200, for defendants Safeway Inc.;
Karen Luong, of Dentons US LLP, Tel. 213.243.6017, for defendants Otis Elevator, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC. Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery (responses to special interrogatories, set one, and requests for production of documents, set one) is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff failed to provide responses within the statutory period and then provided tardy unverified responses after defendant served a motion to compel. Plaintiff has therefore waived his objections pursuant to C.C.P. section 2030.090, which does not cease to apply "merely because a party serves a tardy response." (Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 390, 407-408.) Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2031.300, plaintiff has waived his objections to the demand for production of documents. Defendant's motion is denied as to the form interrogatories, Set one. Plaintiff David Norton shall pay monetary sanctions to defendant in the amount of $950. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
